commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=7af8e48d1e541621cf6b939c574a9b1fa38b0d68)
commands.review_summary:
The PR replaces the diffs viewer toolbar raw SVG string parameter with a typed icon-name map, adds a source-inspection test, and rebuilds the diffs viewer runtime bundle.

Reproducibility: yes. for the source-level sink: current main has iconMarkup: string assigned to button.innerHTML. I did not establish an exploitable user-controlled path because the current callers are hardcoded, matching the linked report's own caveat.
commands.review_followup: The blocker requires contributor action on the open PR plus real behavior proof that ClawSweeper automation cannot supply for the contributor's environment.

target.title: fix(diffs): replace iconMarkup string with ToolbarIconName enum to el…
target.body: ## Summary - Replace `createToolbarButton`'s `iconMarkup: string` parameter with `icon: ToolbarIconName`, a union of 8 known icon name literals - Move SVG generation into a sealed `toolbarIconSvg` map so `innerHTML` only receives compile-time-known strings - Remove 5 old icon functions (`splitIcon`/`unifiedIcon`/`wrapIcon`/`backgroundIcon`/`themeIcon`); callers now pass icon name literals - Rebuild served viewer runtime bundle (`assets/viewer-runtime.js`) to reflect the change - Closes #83918 ## Real behavior proof Behavior addressed: `button.innerHTML = params.iconMarkup` at `viewer-client.ts:146` accepted arbitrary strings, creating an XSS sink. The fix replaces `iconMarkup: string` with `icon: ToolbarIconName` union type, so `innerHTML` now only reads from the sealed `toolbarIconSvg` map — TypeScript rejects raw strings at the call site. Real environment tested: Linux x86_64, Node 22, local checkout of openclaw/openclaw@5091e20 Exact steps or command run after this patch: (1) `node -e "const fs=require('fs');const s=fs.readFileSync('extensions/diffs/src/viewer-client.ts','utf8');console.log('iconMarkup gone:',!s.includes('iconMarkup: string'));console.log('toolbarIconSvg used:',s.includes('toolbarIconSvg[params.icon]'));console.log('no old icon fns:',!s.includes('function splitIcon(')&&!s.includes('function wrapIcon('))"` (2) `npx tsc --noEmit --project extensions/diffs/tsconfig.json` (3) `grep -c iconMarkup extensions/diffs/assets/viewer-runtime.js` (4) `node -e "const{JSDOM}=require('jsdom');const d=new JSDOM('');const b=d.window.document.createElement('button');b.innerHTML='';console.log('XSS baseline onerror injected:',b.innerHTML.includes('onerror'))"` Evidence after fix: Source structural checks all pass: `iconMarkup: string` absent, `toolbarIconSvg[params.icon]` present, old icon functions removed. TypeScript compilation clean. Rebuilt `viewer-runtime.js` contains zero `iconMarkup` references (grep returns 0). XSS baseline confirms `innerHTML` with malicious `` injects successfully, proving the sink existed and our type-level fix closes it. Observed result after fix: The single `innerHTML` assignment at `viewer-client.ts:146` now reads exclusively from `toolbarIconSvg[params.icon]` where `params.icon` is typed as `ToolbarIconName` — a union of 8 string literals. Arbitrary strings are rejected at compile time. The served `viewer-runtime.js` bundle no longer contains the `iconMarkup` parameter. All 8 icon SVGs contain no event handlers (`onerror`, `onclick`, `onload`) or `
